name: CalFresh (SNAP)
why: Household income ($2,200/mo) is below 130% of the Federal Poverty Line for a household of 3 ($2,776/mo). Categorically eligible with children in the household.
documents: Photo ID (driver's license or state ID)
Proof of income (last 30 days of pay stubs)
Proof of residency (utility bill or lease)
Social Security numbers for household members
processingTime: ~30 days · Expedited within 3 days if urgent
applyMethod: Online at GetCalFresh.org, by phone, or at your county office

name: Medi-Cal (Medicaid)
why: Household income is below 138% of the Federal Poverty Line ($2,948/mo for a household of 3). California expanded Medicaid under the ACA.
documents: Proof of income (pay stubs, tax return)
processingTime: ~45 days · Coverage may start on application date
applyMethod: Apply at CoveredCA.com or call the county office

name: Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)
why: Working income under the EITC ceiling with 2 qualifying children. Based on 2024 IRS tables — refund amount depends on exact earnings.
documents: Social Security cards for you and each child
Child's birth certificate or school records
deadline: File by April 15, 2026 (or with an extension)
renewal: Claim each year at tax time
processingTime: Refund typically within 21 days of e-filing
applyMethod: Claim on Form 1040, Schedule EIC · Free filing at IRS.gov/freefile

name: WIC (Women, Infants & Children)
why: Household has a child under age 5 and income is at or below 185% of the Federal Poverty Line ($3,952/mo for a household of 3).
documents: Proof of income (pay stubs or benefits letter)
renewal: Renew every 6–12 months depending on child's age
processingTime: Same-day certification at most WIC clinics
applyMethod: Call your local WIC clinic to schedule an appointment

name: LIHEAP (Energy Assistance)
why: Household income is at or below 150% of the Federal Poverty Line. LIHEAP helps with heating, cooling, and past-due utility bills.
documents: Proof of income for the past 30 days
deadline: Applications open October — funds are first-come, first-served
renewal: Apply again each cooling or heating season
applyMethod: Call your local LIHEAP office or apply through your state's portal
